articles.title: JUICE is planning to do science on Jupiter's 'minor' moons too
articles.description: The European Space Agency's (ESA's) Jupiter Icy Moons Explorer (JUICE) probe is on its (very long) way to Jupiter, and will finally arrive at the King of Planets in 2031. Its primary mission is to focus on the "big three" icy moons—Ganymede, Europa,

articles.title: Gravitational waves leave imprints on light emitted by atoms, theoretical study predicts
articles.description: Gravitational waves are ripples in spacetime produced by violent cosmic events, such as the merging of black holes. So far, direct detections have relied on measuring tiny distance changes over kilometer-scale instruments. In a new theoretical study

articles.title: Live updates: Trump says he expects 'Japan to step up' on Hormuz; Hegseth blasts Europe, media

articles.title: Trump ribs Japanese PM on Pearl Harbor in Oval Office talk on Iran

articles.title: Jeffries says Dems will soon push another resolution to end war in Iran
articles.description: House Democrats will soon launch another effort to end President Trump’s war with Iran, House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.) announced on Thursday. “What we’re focused on now is stopping this reckless war of choice,” Jeffries told reporters

articles.title: The moon's going to get crowded. We should protect our heritage on it while we still can
articles.description: In 1959, the Luna 2 probe from the Soviet Union became the very first human-made object to reach our closest celestial neighbor. In the decades since, we have been leaving footprints—both literally and figuratively—all over the moon. Today, there are

articles.title: In the strait of Hormuz, where are the allies?
articles.description: The Iranian threat to close the Strait of Hormuz has caused world oil prices to spike, and the US Navy is preparing for mine-countermeasure operations, but needs the help of allies to augment its capabilities and secure the sea lanes.

articles.title: ECB Tells Banks to Identify the Leverage Providers on SRT Deals
articles.description: The European Central Bank is probing the use of leverage underpinning banks’ fast-growing significant risk transfer market, according to people familiar with the matter.

articles.title: Faisal Islam: Iran war is having a dramatic effect on the UK economy
articles.description: The knock-on effects of the war in the Gulf go beyond a hold on interest rates and are set to reverberate for months.

articles.title: 'Mini earthquakes' turn tiny chips into radio signal powerhouses

articles.title: Chinese Founder of Router-Maker TP-Link Seeks a Trump Gold Card
articles.description: The Chinese founder of the massive router maker facing national security probes by the Trump administration has applied for an expedited visa under the Trump Gold Card program, according to people familiar with the matter.

articles.title: Quantum Firms Xanadu, Quantum Horizon to List as Capital Needs Outweigh Stocks Rout
articles.description: Quantum computing companies are braving turbulent public markets in order to raise money to develop the still experimental technology, with two more taking critical steps toward debuts this week alone.

articles.title: Oak Hill Bets on Private Credit Rebound With Retail Fund Launch
articles.description: Oak Hill Advisors is courting retail investors who have grown increasingly skeptical of the $1.8 trillion private credit market with the launch of a new fund that will deploy capital across public and private debt.

articles.title: Japan, Europeans signal 'readiness to contribute' to efforts to keep Strait of Hormuz open

articles.title: Japan PM to hold 'difficult' talks with Trump as Iran conflict looms large
articles.description: It comes days after Trump criticised Japan for not answering his demands for help in the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Hospital waiting lists in Wales see record drop
articles.description: Wait times for planned care are falling but diagnostic and cancers waits are up, latest figures indicate.

articles.title: US Household Net Worth Climbs $2.2 Trillion to Fresh Record
articles.description: US household wealth rose in the fourth quarter to a fresh record as the stock market continued to rally as the year drew to a close.

articles.title: Hospital order for man who stalked Myleene Klass
articles.description: Peter Windsor is sentenced after sending items including an air pistol to Klass and another presenter.

articles.title: ECB Officials See Possibility of Rate Hike at April Meeting
articles.description: European Central Bank policymakers would be ready to raise interest rates as soon as their next meeting should fallout from the war in Iran push inflation too far above target, according to people familiar with the situation.

articles.title: Average US long-term mortgage rate rises to 6.22%, highest level in more than 3 months
articles.description: Average US long-term mortgage rate rises to 6.22%, highest level in more than 3 months  AP News

articles.title: Paris mayoral race: Socialist frontrunner faces fierce attacks from rivals

articles.title: Why Apple wants you to update your iPhone, iPad now
articles.description: If you haven't updated your Apple device in a while, now may be the time to do it.

articles.title: ‘Stock’s on Sale’: Power CEO Orr Urges Investors to Buy the Dip
articles.description: The departing head of Power Corp. of Canada said the company’s falling share price represents a buying opportunity, as the value of its holdings is much higher.

articles.title: Watch: Missile lands next to presenter during live report from Lebanon
articles.description: In footage from Russian state broadcaster RT, correspondent Steve Sweeney is seen diving off screen as a missile hits.

articles.title: Ancient brines helped build Idaho's Silver Valley and Cobalt belt

articles.title: The Hugglescote Death Star in Hugglescote, England
articles.description:
When building a new estate in the village of Hugglescote in Leicestershire, the constructors gave a nod to “Star Wars” by creating a roundabout which when viewed from above was a representation of the Death Star.

articles.title: Mortgage Rates Climb to Three-Month High, With 30-Year at 6.22%
articles.description: Mortgage rates rose for a third week, reaching a three-month high as wartime inflation fears drove up yields for the government bonds that guide home loans.

articles.title: Meta to Reduce Role of Outside Content Moderators in Favor of AI
articles.description: Meta Platforms Inc. will soon cut back on its use of third-party vendors to help with content moderation, relying instead on advanced artificial intelligence systems to detect and remove posts that violate the company’s terms of service.

articles.title: Smart sanctions can save Trump's 'peace through strength' plan

articles.title: After 25 years, Valve reworks Counter-Strike's reload system
articles.description: Full-magazine reloads throw out muscle memory in favor of "higher stakes" decisions.

articles.title: UK to Refurbish Colonial-Era Nigeria Port Built a Century Ago
articles.description: Britain agreed to help refurbish Nigeria’s two busiest ports, including one built a century ago when the West African nation was ruled from London.

articles.title: Brazil Finance Minister Fernando Haddad Steps Down Ahead of Elections
articles.description: Brazil Finance Minister Fernando Haddad said he will step down on Thursday, before an April deadline for officials to leave their positions to run for elected office.

articles.title: Stock markets rattled and prices soar after strikes on Qatar gas hub
articles.description: Energy costs jump after fresh strikes hit key infrastructure in the Middle East.

articles.title: What is the UK Covid inquiry and how does it work?
articles.description: The third report from the inquiry into the government's handling of the pandemic says the NHS was close to collapse.

articles.title: Janus Living IPO Said to Hit $840 Million, Price at Top of Range
articles.description: Janus Living Inc., a seniors-focused real estate investment trust, is expected to increase the size of its initial public offering to $840 million and price it at the top of the marketed range, according to people familiar with the matter.

articles.title: Study pinpoints when bow and arrow came to North America
articles.description: Radiocarbon results suggest a single origin and rapid diffusion through cultural transition networks.

articles.title: Accor Denies Involvement in Child Trafficking Following Report
articles.description: Accor SA opened a probe following a short-seller report that questioned the group’s human rights practices, saying it denies “involvement in the alleged systemic exploitation of human or child trafficking.”

articles.title: Blackstone Raises $12 Billion for Asia PE Fund as It Nears Close
articles.description: Blackstone Inc. gathered more than $12 billion for its latest Asia-Pacific buyout fund, as the alternative investment manager bets on India, Japan and Australia to help it boost private equity returns.

articles.title: Fernandez fully committed to Chelsea - Rosenior
articles.description: Chelsea boss Liam Rosenior says midfielder Enzo Fernandez is "fully committed" to the club after the midfielder appeared to cast doubt on his future.

articles.title: Three Palestinian women killed during Iranian missile attack that struck beauty salon
articles.description: Others were injured when the salon in the southern West Bank was hit, while a Thai worker was killed by falling shrapnel in Israel.

articles.title: 'Movement never lies': 100 years of the Martha Graham Dance Company
articles.description: Graham was a creative force in the performing arts. She wanted dance to express authentic, human emotions — a revolutionary idea in the late 1920s.
